description: References TeamCreate(team_name='production-grade') and TeamDelete(team_name='production-grade') which are not built-in Claude Code tools; the Full Build Pipeline team lifecycle will fail at these call sites
suggested_fix: Replace TeamCreate/TeamDelete with a documented agent-management pattern or remove team lifecycle calls; document which tool provides these if from a third-party extension

description: TeamDelete(team_name='production-grade') in Pipeline Cleanup section — same non-existent tool as TeamCreate; cleanup step will error or no-op
suggested_fix: Remove or replace with documented cleanup mechanism

description: Lists smart_outline and smart_search as tools to use for codebase understanding; neither is a built-in Claude Code tool
suggested_fix: Replace with standard tools (Glob, Grep, Read) or document which plugin provides smart_outline/smart_search

description: Fallback protocol says 'Use smart_outline before full Read' — smart_outline is not a standard Claude Code tool
suggested_fix: Replace smart_outline with the Read tool with a limit parameter for partial file reads

description: Auto-update instructs Claude to mkdir ~/.claude/plugins/cache/nagisanzenin/production-grade/{remote_version} and cp -r downloaded code into it, then overwrite ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json — system-directory writes without hash or signature verification of retrieved code
suggested_fix: Add SHA256 verification of cloned content against a pinned hash in plugin.json before copying; warn user that system files will be modified

description: Version check issues WebFetch to raw.githubusercontent.com and trusts the response unconditionally; a compromised CDN or MITM could serve a malicious version string
suggested_fix: Pin the version check URL to a specific release tag or verify the response against a known-good hash

description: git clone --depth 1 from GitHub to /tmp/pg-update fetches arbitrary code; cloned content is subsequently copied to ~/.claude/ without integrity verification
suggested_fix: Verify the cloned commit SHA against a pinned expected value before copying; consider using a tagged release download with a checksum

evidence: Fallback path '$HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/nagisanzenin/production-grade' has no version component in the directory path
description: Hook fallback path has no version component; auto-update installs to a versioned subdirectory so the unversioned fallback may point to a missing or stale session-guard.sh when CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is unset
suggested_fix: Add version suffix to fallback path or resolve installPath from installed_plugins.json at hook execution time

evidence: 'sensible defaults' appears 7+ times, 'appropriate' 5+ times, 'relevant' 4+ times across the file
description: Vague quantifiers throughout the orchestrator skill; penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Replace 'sensible defaults' with the actual defaults (e.g., Standard engagement + Sequential parallelism); replace 'appropriate' with the specific condition being tested

evidence: 'Are appropriate levels used (error for errors, warn for degraded...)' — 'appropriate' used as the criterion before the definition
description: 'appropriate' used as a gate word before the specification is given; 2-3 further occurrences across the file
suggested_fix: Reorder to state the criterion first: 'Use error for errors, warn for degraded, info for business events, debug for troubleshooting'

evidence: 'sensible defaults', 'reasonable' and 'appropriate' each appear in Express/Standard mode descriptions without specifying actual values
description: Three distinct vague quantifiers used in engagement-mode behavior table without specifying the concrete defaults they refer to
suggested_fix: Specify the actual default values: e.g., 'Express: React + Tailwind + Zustand' instead of 'sensible defaults for framework, styling, state management'

evidence: 'relevant artifacts', 'appropriate depth', 'good options' used as undefined criteria
description: Three distinct vague quantifiers in dialogue protocol and gate companion sections
suggested_fix: Specify what makes options 'good' (specific, not generic; cover likely directions) — the file does define this elsewhere but not at the point of use

evidence: 'appropriate error level', 'sensible defaults', 'relevant phase file' each appear without definition
description: Three vague quantifiers in engagement-mode and fallback protocol sections
suggested_fix: Specify the defaults: e.g., 'Express: auto-resolve all decisions, report in completion summary' instead of 'Sensible defaults for all implementation choices'

evidence: Architecture fitness function uses 'sensible', 'appropriate' as criteria in table cells
description: Vague quantifiers in the fitness-function derivation table; the table's purpose is to be prescriptive but uses qualitative language
suggested_fix: Use concrete architecture names and thresholds in the fitness table cells

evidence: 'appropriate' (2 occurrences), 'relevant' in interview and verification sections
description: Minor vague quantifier usage; file is otherwise specific
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate level of detail' with the specific depth for each engagement mode

evidence: Express mode: 'Generate all test suites with sensible coverage targets'
description: 'sensible coverage targets' is undefined; Express mode should specify the actual default thresholds (e.g., 80% lines)
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Generate all test suites with 80% line / 75% branch coverage targets (Express defaults)'

evidence: Phase 5 and README template hardcode 'nagisanzenin' as the marketplace owner and author
description: Generated plugin READMEs and marketplace entries embed the plugin author's username; adopters who use skill-maker will produce artifacts with another person's identity
suggested_fix: Replace hardcoded 'nagisanzenin' references with a template variable resolved from git config user.name or via an interview question

evidence: 'sensible defaults for all infra', 'appropriate' in Express mode
description: Two vague quantifiers in engagement-mode behavior table
suggested_fix: Specify the cloud provider default (e.g., 'AWS with ECS Fargate + RDS Aurora') for Express mode

evidence: 'appropriate' and 'relevant' in engagement mode and scope boundary sections
description: Two vague quantifiers; file is otherwise highly specific with STRIDE/OWASP methodology
suggested_fix: Minor: replace 'appropriate risk tolerance' with 'ask: Critical only / all High / all Medium'

description: Dispatches to phases/01-system-audit.md through phases/06-cost-modeling.md; these sub-files were not in the audit scope and their absence would cause skill failure at dispatch step. Same pattern in frontend-engineer, software-engineer, solution-architect, security-engineer, sre, technical-writer skills.
suggested_fix: Verify phase files are present in the installed plugin; add a phase-file existence check to the production-readiness gate

description: plugin.json does not enumerate bundled skills; if the Claude Code plugin loader uses a skills array for registration, new skills added to disk but not to plugin.json would go unregistered
suggested_fix: Add a 'skills' array to plugin.json listing all 14 skill directory paths
